revision.text:
[[ja:天香山命]][[zh:天香山命]] {{Infobox deity|name=Amenokaguyama no Mikoto (天香山命)|predecessor=|successor=|type={{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Amatsukami|1=Amatsukami|2=zh|3=天神 (日本神話)|4=fr|5=Amatsukami|6=mni|7=ꯑꯃꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ|comment=confirming this goes to Amatsukami|1=Amatsukami|2=zh|3=天神 (日本神話)|4=fr|5=Amatsukami|6=mni|7=ꯑꯃꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}|full_name=Amenokaguyama no Mikoto|other_names=Amenokagoyama no Mikoto, Amenokagoyama no Kami, Amenokagoyama no Mikoto
Also known as {{ill|Takakuraji|en|Takakuraji|qid=Q11668757|lt=Takakuraji|1=Takakuraji}}|alias=|Rank=|divine_attributes=|tomb=|father={{ill|Amenohoakari|en|Amenohoakari|qid=Q10940411|lt=Amenokaguyama no Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}} or {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}|mother={{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=}}|relatives=|spouse=Hoya Hime|children={{ill|Amenomurakumo no Mikoto|ja|大沼主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenomurakumo no Mikoto|1=Ame no Murakumo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q106241464}}|shrines={{ill|Yahiko Shrine|en|Yahiko Shrine|qid=Q195684|lt=Yahiko Shrine|1=Yahiko Shrine}}, {{ill|Uonuma Shrine|ja|魚沼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uonuma Shrine|qq=}}, among others|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|parents={{ill|honoakari|qid=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}}} {{One source|date=April 2019|qq=}} {{No footnotes|date=April 2019|qq=}} {{Infobox Japanese deity | Name = Amekaguyama-no-Mikoto | Predecessor = | Successor = | Divine classification = [[Amatsukami]] | Full name = Amekaguyama-no-Mikoto | alternate_name = Amenokagoyama-no-Mikoto, Amenokagowoyama-no-Mikoto, Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto
Also known as [[Takakuraji]] | Other names = | Rank = | Attributes = | Burial place = | Father = [[Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto]] or [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] | Mother = [[Amato Hime-no-Mikoto]] | Relatives = | Spouse = Hoya-hime | Children = [[Amanomurakumo-no-Mikoto|Amanomurakumo]] | Shrine = [[Yahiko Shrine]], [[Uonuma Shrine]], and others }} '''Amekaguyama-no-Mikoto''' (天香山命) is a deity appearing in [[Japanese mythology]]. It is said to be another name for {{ill|Takakuraji|en|Takakuraji|qid=Q11668757|lt=Takakuraji|1=Takakuraji}}.『[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1913333/1/35 神道大辞典 1]』 p.49（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション） [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1913333/1/35 "Shintō Daijiten 1"], p.49 (National Diet Library Digital Collection) == Overview == The name may also be written as '''Amenokagoyama-no-Mikoto''' (天香語山命) or '''Amenokagowoyama-no-Mikoto''' (天賀吾山命), and is pronounced as "Ame(ma)-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto." According to the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}, this deity was born to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|饒速日尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}} (identified with {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|Amenohoakari|qid=Q10940411|lt=Amenohoakari-no-Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}} in the text) and {{ill|Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amato Hime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, making it a great-grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}. It is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Owari clan|en|Owari clan|qid=Q11465311|lt=Owari clan|1=Owari clan}}, while {{ill|Umashimaji|ja|宇摩志摩治命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|qid=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan|1=Mononobe clan}}, is its half-sibling through a different mother. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|qid=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} also mentions this deity, listing clans such as the Owari clan (Sakyō Shimbetsu), {{ill|Iokibe clan|ja|伊福部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iokibe clan|qq=}} (Sakyō Shimbetsu Lower), {{ill|Muraji clan|ja|六人部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muraji clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Yamashiro Shimbetsu), and {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori clan|qq=}} (Settsu Shimbetsu) as its descendants. The "Tenson Hongi" section of the Sendai Kuji Hongi describes how this deity, named Takuri Hiko-no-Mikoto (手栗彦命) or Takakuraji-no-Mikoto (高倉下命), resided in Kumano-no-Sato in {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|qid=Q864053|lt=Kii Province|1=Kii Province}} (modern {{ill|Shingū, Wakayama|en|Shingū, Wakayama|qid=Q823526|lt=Shingū, Wakayama|1=Shingū, Wakayama}} and the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀ノ川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀の川|1=Kinokawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1042624}} area). The text recounts a story matching the "Takakuraji" tradition found in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} and {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} (see {{ill|Takakuraji|en|Takakuraji|qid=Q11668757|lt=Takakuraji|1=Takakuraji}} for more details). The {{ill|Yahiko Shrine|en|Yahiko Shrine|qid=Q195684|lt=Yahiko Shrine|1=Yahiko Shrine}} tradition in {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|en|Niigata Prefecture|qid=Q132705|lt=Niigata Prefecture|1=Niigata Prefecture}} claims that after {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}'s conquest of the Yamato region, this deity was tasked with pacifying and cultivating the Koshi region (see {{ill|Yahiko Shrine|en|Yahiko Shrine|qid=Q195684|lt=Yahiko Shrine|1=Yahiko Shrine}} for details). == Overview == Amenokaguyama no Mikoto is also written as '''Amenokagoyama no Mikoto''' and '''Amenokagoyama no Kami,''' and may be pronounced as "Amenokagoyama no Mikoto." According to the ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}},'' he is a descendant of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, born to {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|饒速日尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}} (equated with {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|Amenohoakari|qid=Q10940411|lt=Amenokaguyama no Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}} in ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'') and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天道日女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=}}. He is thus a great-grandchild of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}. He is regarded as an ancestral deity of the {{ill|Owari clan|en|Owari clan|qid=Q11465311|lt=Owari clan|1=Owari clan}}, and as a sibling deity to {{ill|Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji no Mikoto|ja|宇摩志摩治命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|qid=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan|1=Mononobe clan}}, through a different mother. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|qid=Q1045362|lt=New Selection of Surnames|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' lists the Owari clan (of Sakyo Shinbetsu origin) as his descendants, along with clans such as the {{ill|Iokibe clan|ja|伊福部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iokibe clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Rokunobe clan|ja|六人部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokunobe clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori clan|qq=}}. In the "Tenson Honki" of ''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' he is counted among the 32 deities who accompanied {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|饒速日尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}} during his {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en|Tenson kōrin|qid=Q1084733|lt=descent to earth|1=Tenson kōrin}}. It states that he resided in the village of Kumano in {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|qid=Q864053|lt=Kii Province|1=Kii Province}} (modern-day {{ill|Shingū, Wakayama|en|Shingū, Wakayama|qid=Q823526|lt=Shingu City|1=Shingū, Wakayama}} in Wakayama Prefecture or the Kinokawa River region) and was also known as "Takuri Hiko no Mikoto" or "Takakuraji no Mikoto." This account parallels the legend of {{ill|Takakuraji|en|Takakuraji|qid=Q11668757|lt=Takakuraji|1=Takakuraji}} found in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} (refer to the article on {{ill|Takakuraji|en|Takakuraji|qid=Q11668757|lt=Takakuraji|1=Takakuraji}} for details). Additionally, the traditions of {{ill|Yahiko Shrine|en|Yahiko Shrine|qid=Q195684|lt=Yahiko Shrine|1=Yahiko Shrine}} in Niigata Prefecture state that after {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} pacified the Yamato region, Amenokaguyama no Mikoto received an imperial order to pacify and develop the Echigo region (modern Niigata Prefecture). (See the article on {{ill|Yahiko Shrine|en|Yahiko Shrine|qid=Q195684|lt=Yahiko Shrine|1=Yahiko Shrine}} for further details.) == Shrines Worshiping This Deity == * {{ill|Yahiko Shrine|en|Yahiko Shrine|qid=Q195684|lt=Yahiko Shrine|1=Yahiko Shrine}} * {{ill|Uonuma Shrine|ja|魚沼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uonuma Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Owari-to Shrine|ja|尾張戸神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari-to Shrine|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == Bibliography == * Miyaji Naoichi and Saeki Ariyoshi (Eds.), *Shintō Daijiten (Shrunken Edition)*, Rin'ensha, 1969, ISBN 4-653-01347-0 (original publication by Heibonsha, 1937) == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|en|List of Japanese deities|qid=Q3240847|lt=List of Japanese Deities|1=List of Japanese deities}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|en|List of Japanese deities|qid=Q3240847|lt=List of Japanese deities|1=List of Japanese deities}} {{Shinto Horizontal|qq=}} {{shinto-stub|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|天香山命|version=104250760|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:天香山命]] [[zh:天香山命]] {{wikidata link|Q10940849}} [[Category:Amatsukami]] [[Category:Japanese deities]]
